text: The laparoscopic camera provides a view of the abdominal cavity, where the surgeon places 5mm ports in the left lower quadrant and suprapubic area. The appendix is revealed and identified, then ligated and excised using surgical instruments.

The surgeon ligates the appendix twice using an end-loop with a number zero V-create, completing the appendectomy. The appendix is extracted through the ten-millimeter camera port. The abdominal cavity and small intestine are explored and visualized, revealing a Meckel's diverticulum in the terminal ileum. A five-millimeter port in the bottom left quadrant is replaced with a twelve-millimeter port at the same incision site.

The surgeon excises the Meckel's diverticulum using a linear laparoscopic stapler. The excised diverticulum is removed through the twelve-millimeter port. The operation concludes with bleeding control and aspiration of the abdominal cavity.
